---
title: "Overview"
---

::: {.hero}
# SAM x Nano4M

A COM-304 project extending nano4M with **SAM segmentation tokens** as a fifth modality. The project asks a simple question: can a compact multimodal model learn segmentation tokens jointly with RGB, depth, normals, and text in a unified masked modeling framework?
:::

::: {.metric-grid}
::: {.metric-card}
### 5
Modalities jointly modeled: RGB, depth, normals, text, and SAM tokens.
:::
::: {.metric-card}
### 50k
Training scenes, each rendered with 10 augmentations.
:::
::: {.metric-card}
### 1.63
Validation cross-entropy for SAM tokens.
:::
::: {.metric-card}
### 38'147
Training steps in the reported run.
:::
:::

## Key takeaways

- SAM tokens can be integrated into nano4M without changing the core model architecture.
- The model is trained **from scratch**, not warm-started from a previous checkpoint.
- The “offset” in this project refers to the **token index range in the data representation**, not offset training.
- SAM reaches competitive loss relative to the other visual modalities on synthetic CLEVR scenes.

## What was added

The extension adds a SAM-based segmentation modality to the existing multiCLEVR pipeline. RGB scenes are pseudo-labeled with SAM 2.1, converted into instance masks, normalized into fixed-length token sequences, and loaded alongside the original modalities.

## What this website shows

This site complements the report with a shorter narrative, result snapshots, and a clearer explanation of what the extension adds to nano4M. It is designed to be self-contained, so a reader can understand the high-level idea without opening the PDF first.
